Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Sep-15-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Motion for an Order to Apportion and Distribute Settlement Funds.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey,CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Geoff Sloniker, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP appeared for plaintiffs.
Richard Richardson, Esq. appeared with plaintiff/successor-in-interest Bobby Phillips, who was placed under oath and testified.
Both Messrs. Phillips and Richardson appeared via CourtCall for this morning's proceedings.
Brenda Beasley, a witness for Mr. Phillips, was placed under oath but did not testify.
Wrongful Death Heir Marc Beasley was present in pro per, placed under oath and testified.
Michelle Beasley and Anthony Woods, witnesses for Mr. Beasley, were placed under oath but did not testify.

The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, RULES that the following are entitled to distribution of all net funds obtained by their counsel, Brayton Purcell LLP, derived from all funds negotiated after the September 18, 2018 death of Henry Beasley in the following proportions:

Estate of Henry Beasley, Deceased - 50%
Marc Beasley, as Wrongful Death Heir - 50%

All future net funds derived from settlements entered into after the September 18, 2018 death of Henry Beasley shall be distributed in the percentages set forth above.

The signature of plaintiff Bobby Phillips shall be sufficient on all settlement documents for the purpose of settling both the estate and wrongful death claims with defendants in all claims with settlements negotiated after Henry Beasley's death.

Counsel for plaintiffs shall prepare the proposed Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds and submit same to the Court for reveiw and signature.

Off Calendar May-11-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only.)
Off Calendar Mar-14-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Mar-14-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jason M. Rose of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and moving party Henry Beasley

RULING:
No other appearances and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition filed. The trial date is July 2, 2018 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is July 12, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is June 28, 2018. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than May 18, 2018. Responses to the demand are due no later than June 1, 2018. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by June 25, 2018 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on June 28, 2018. Fact discovery cut-off date is June 15, 2018. Expert discovery cut-off date is June 29, 2018. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than April 13, 2018. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than March 28, 2018 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than March 28, 2018 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on May 16, 2018 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to CRC, Rule 3.1380 and San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rules 5.0 and 20.2 on July 2, 2018 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rules 5.0 and 20.2 and provide a confidential settlement statement by June 28, 2018 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Lynn O'Malley-Taylor, matter not reported. (503/LOT)
